steps.title: Convert percentage increase to growth factor
steps.content:
When population increases by 4% per year, each year's population is the previous year's population plus 4% of it.

$$\text{New population} = \text{Old} + 0.04 \cdot \text{Old} = \text{Old} \times 1.04$$

The growth factor is 1.04.

steps.title: Recognize exponential growth pattern
steps.content:
Starting from 15,000 in 2005 (x = 0):
  • After 1 year: $15{,}000 \times 1.04$
  • After 2 years: $15{,}000 \times 1.04 \times 1.04 = 15{,}000 \times (1.04)^2$
  • After x years: $15{,}000 \times (1.04)^x$

steps.title: Write the equation and match the choice
steps.content:
Combining the initial population and the exponential growth pattern gives:
$$p = 15{,}000(1.04)^x$$

Comparing with the four choices, this matches choice D — D is correct.
